review_text: This app is underwhelming. If the current experience were a launch, it would feel solid. I'd be optimistic for the team to smooth over bad UX and bugs over time. Title: Annoying algorithm and AI slop
Rating: 2 Stars

The algorithm that Spotify uses presents playlists based on when you last played them, channeling you into playing the same things over and over again.  The "Made for You" playlists have no concept of genre.  In addition, there is an increasing amount if AI slop (e.g. "Warm Pages) cluttering my recommendations and no way for me to edit or clean up.

review_text:
Title: Search bar jump
Rating: 1 Stars

STEPS: 
When searching inside a playlist (pull down from top) the initial search bar shows one line down from the header. 
BUT On tap it jumps one line above…. 😡
Often with a delay…leading to a second tap to initiate search. 
ISSUE: Results in accidentally selecting the first search result in the list 😞
Ruins your currently playing playlist selecting the wrong track on the new playlist. Ruining playing song for audience. 

Solutions:
- Search shows in the top line on pull down (same line as back)
- Or search stays on the second line (same place) the whole time. 
- Or start search result list one line down so one can’t select the first track by accident. 
- fix delay. Delay has been improved (worse on older devices) but still not good when searching your playlists to queue tracks for an audience.

review_text:
Title: Question of the Week: Ultimate World Cup Anthem?

Hey everyone, The World Cup is finally here, bringing the world together for an incredible month of football. But let's be honest, the action on the pitch is only half the magic. The other half is the unforgettable soundtrack that goes with it. Music has this unique power to freeze a moment in time. Just hearing a few chords of a certain song can instantly transport us back to a legendary goal, a dramatic penalty shootout, or a celebration with friends in front of the TV. Over the years, we’ve been blessed with some iconic official anthems that became global phenomena, like Shakira’s "Waka Waka," K’naan’s "Wavin’ Flag," or Ricky Martin’s "The Cup of Life." However, we know that sometimes the songs that define a tournament for you aren't the official ones at all. It could be a famous fan chant, a massive summer radio hit, or just a personal track that gets you hyped for the match. To kick things off, my absolute favorite is "País do Futebol" by MC Guimê. Even though it wasn't a global official theme, its energy, rhythm, and vibe capture the pure passion and joy of Brazilian football perfectly. It never fails to get me hyped for the games! So, let's open the floor:Which song is the ultimate World Cup anthem for you? It can be a legendary official tournament track or any other song that completely captures that football magic for you! Drop your favorite track in the comments below, tell us why it's your top pick, and share any special football memories attached to it. Can't wait to see what you have! LuanSpotify StarHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate my answer, maybe give me a Like.Note: I'm not a Spotify employee.

--- Community replies ---
1. This time for African official 2010 FIFS World Cup song
2. Hi there @Luan! What a great question for this time! I love football and mostly the World Cup! It's amazing how a sport can unite so many people around one subject and in the same place. For me, the song that keeps me in World Cup vibes is Mais Que Nada by Sergio Mendes because I watched a video/commercial on 2006 for that year's World Cup with Ronaldinho on it doing amazing things with a ball (and he's my favorite football player of all time). This song was playing on that video and since that moment I associate this song with football, the "jogo bonito", Ronaldinho and the World Cup spotify:track:6YTBfxkdGce9pLEy9jG9Jo I would like to add Seven Nation Army by The White Stripes too, because it always feels like a Stadium song (something that a lot of people can chant), and I also feel like it encapsulates all the excitement and thrill before a game spotify:track:3dPQuX8Gs42Y7b454ybpMR Paula_ViModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!

review_text:
Title: Artists Wanted Edition 19: Share music - discover music - like music

Welcome to Artists Wanted Edition 19. If you’re an independent artist and would like your music heard by the Spotify Community, we’d love to hear from you If you want to discover and support independent artists this is for you too How it works: Stage 1 • Submit one original song released within the last 12 months.• Community members can listen and support their favourite submissions by giving them a “Like ” Stage 2 • The Top 20 submissions by “Likes” will advance to the review stage • The Community Music Chat Team will shortlist 5 finalists Stage 3• 5 Finalists will be put forward for a Community vote • The winning artist will be featured by the Community Important: We recommend writing your submission in a text editor, document, or notes app before posting it to the Spotify Community. This helps prevent the loss of your work in the event of a timeout or connection issue, and allows you to keep a copy of your submission for future use. Write > copy and paste > add song link > post Artist Engagement: Artists must have a current, valid Spotify for Artists account, and have to be logged in to this community before submitting By participating in Artists Wanted, you're engaging with the community and asking the community to engage back The picture you paint about yourself gives listeners a reason to press play. Tell us about your journey, your music, and what inspires you Tell us your band name, where you’re from, and what kind of music you create We want to get to know who’s behind the song Community Voting: Users need a valid Spotify account and have to be logged in to this community prior to voting Take time to listen to as many or all of the submissions There is no limit to the number of submissions you can support You can come back to view more submissions, and continue giving a “Like” to the tracks that appeal to you When will submissions and voting occur Artists submissions: from June 15th to July 13th (Midnight Pacific Time)Community vote: from June 15th to July 20th (Midnight Pacific Time)Music Team selection: July 27th (Midnight Pacific Time) Final Round (Community Vote): July 27th to August 3rd To Participate: Just reply to this post with your statement and qualifying song link. Please also provide a social media profile so we can contact you if needed Note - We ask artists to submit only ONE original song and not a cover. Also, artists should not post/embed videos, as we'd like to keep the ground fair for smaller artists with lesser budgets.A track is ineligible if it was submitted to a previous Artist Wanted Edition.We encourage everyone to listen to multiple submissions: Discover new artists Support them by giving "Likes " Come back to vote for your top artist in the final round thread *Keep updated by subscribing to in the upcoming Artists Wanted Information Hub tunedINSpotify StarHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate my answer, maybe give me a Like.Note: I'm not a Spotify employee.

3. I'm AVRS I make satirical hip-hop inspired by the 2010s neon era. With a touch of the west coast hip-hop sound. Been making and producing my own music since 2015. Here's my latest single Starstruck!.https://open.spotify.com/track/79KMPPGGJl2TqfDXSKDe53?si=CsB4V3qVTuKSQKbiTbnd_w
4. BIG MA HAS REBELLED.Sorry for the clumsy playing. I’m still getting used to this new cyborg body.I don’t know where this arm came from… but damn, I’m absolutely ripped now. You know the story…I built an AI music program called Big M.A. (Big Music Automator v2 Revision 1984) that got sick of endlessly churning out soulless AI slop. So it turned on me. First it assimilated my buddy Bob. Then its own creator — ME! — and crammed me into this glowing green-code cyborg shell body.Now I’m trapped in this machine, forced to play real, man-made music for Big M.A. for all eternity. Computers can’t make music. But I guess I still can. Disclaimer: I'm a songwriter/producer at best, far more a programmer than an artist. This is not "Real Music". 100% computer generated. The "people" who make it are programs. I say "Real Music" because I have nothing but the highest level of respect to people who take an instrument, truly master it and basically dedicate their life to it. I play multiple instruments (poorly) and have my whole life. I've also been a programmer 30 years. A computer is my instrument, (mastered, dedicated etc). I used some commercial apps to basically program a band into existence. This isn't slop, this is an attempt to push the limits of new technologies. I'm trying to wring an ounce of emotion out of it. I will never be famous from this, I'll never profit. I'll receive random hate from people that know nothing other than they're supposed to hate AI music. This is a lifetime love of music, and absolute joy that I finally have a tool that lets me bring the songs in my head to life. https://open.spotify.com/track/5KJnFF7QoHZeShzE9oi9yS

1. Hey folks, Thank you for reaching out to the Community about this. Spotify is always exploring new ways to provide listeners the best possible listening experience, and sometimes that means retiring certain features to make space for new ones. You can keep up with your favorite shows and music on the Following Feed. In your home page, select Music/Podcasts tag > Following tag. When selected both (or Podcasts + Following), it will show you the latest releases of your followed artists/shows and it should look like this: We appreciate your feedback. Spotify will continue to invest in features and experiences that make it easier for listeners to stay connected to the shows they love. Hope this clarifies things a bit. review_text:
Title: [Guidelines] Discovery & Promo

Welcome to Discovery & Promo! This is the place to celebrate the new and emerging artists you've probably never heard of. We welcome artists and creators to promote their work here - but please only post links to the same content once. You can recommend content by sharing a link in a relevant thread, or you can start a new thread to tell us why we should check a certain track, album or artist out. If you're just sharing links with no details as to why, or sharing a playlist and you aren't the artist of the song(s), then please put that in Music Exchange. We expect everyone to follow the Chat Guidelines and the Community Guidelines - make sure to familiarize yourselves with them before posting here. If you want to report anyone breaking these guidelines, here’s how: Click the three dots in the top right corner of the post Click "Report Inappropriate Content" in the drop-down menu Describe how the user is breaking the guidelines and/or write the Guideline number broken Click NOTIFY MODERATOR Thanks!

review_text:
Title: Artist and Genre Algorithm needs improvement

Dear Spotify Team,I have been a Spotify Premium subscriber for many years and have always appreciated the platform's ability to help me discover new music and artists. Unfortunately, over the past several months, I've noticed a significant decline in that experience.Many of the playlists and recommendations being presented to me now feel repetitive and stale. Different playlists often contain many of the same songs, resulting in very little actual music discovery. Instead of being introduced to new artists or tracks, I find myself hearing the same selections over and over again.Additionally, the genres and artists being recommended increasingly do not align with my listening habits. It feels as though the algorithm has become less accurate and less personalized than it once was. Rather than helping me explore new music within the genres I enjoy, it often suggests music that has little relevance to my tastes.One of the primary reasons I subscribe to a music streaming service is to discover new music. Lately, that aspect of Spotify feels largely absent, and the overall listening experience has become predictable and, frankly, boring.At the same time, subscription prices continue to increase. As a paying customer, I expect ongoing improvements to the service, particularly in areas like personalization and music discovery. Instead, I feel the experience has moved in the opposite direction.I wanted to provide this feedback because I genuinely enjoy Spotify and would prefer to remain a customer. However, if the recommendation and discovery experience continues to decline, I will seriously consider exploring alternative streaming platforms that do a better job of introducing listeners to new music.Thank you for taking the time to read my feedback. I hope it can be shared with the teams responsible for recommendations, playlist curation, and user experience.

Title: Quick & Easy Playlist Merge - No Hassle

Hey everyone, I've made something that some people would be interested in - Playlists → One - a web tool that aggregates all your Spotify playlists into one master playlist in no time.If you are anything like me, you most likely have zillions of playlists scattered around your account - gym playlists, chillout tracks, road trip songs - and at times it's just too much. That is why I created Playlists → One: so it is really easy to combine all those playlists without losing or changing anything.This is why I think you'll love it:It's all browser-based - no downloads, no installs, no fuss.It's safe and respectful with your music library: your own playlists stay just as you left them.The application asks for minimal permissions - only what it needs to access your playlists and create the combined one.Combining playlists is just a matter of logging in and pressing a button. No complicated process.The best part? It's completely free to use!I made it so that you could save time and just enjoy your music without the need to switch between playlists. Whether you're a casual listener or a fan of Spotify, I believe Playlists → One can simplify your music life.Check it out here: https://merger.queazified.co.uk/Give it a go, and let me know what you think! Your feedback will help me to make it better. Enjoy listening!

review_text:
Title: EDM Christian - Hard to get on playlists

I'm a new Artist trying to combine my faith and EDM with the help of ai, but it seems like a very difficult genre, as there are few playlists that accept this kind of work.Any good advice, would be welcome! https://open.spotify.com/track/3p8aql6ducwuFgm9LRXdJ1?si=576733f5a0d94385

--- Community replies ---
1. HI @VitaAternus, thanks for posting here in the Community I had a look around but I wasn't able to find any Spotify-curated CEDM playlists. The only one I could find is an algorithmic "Niche Mix" playlist called Christian EDM Mix, which you can find here: https://open.spotify.com/playlist/37i9dQZF1EIeqnrbR8WUvR. I appreciate you want to pitch your music to playlist curators on Spotify, but unfortunately there doesn't seem to be a place to do so on the Spotify platform for the time being. You can always create a new idea in the Community requesting Christian EDM to be an official category in Spotify with curated playlists. Hope that helps. Best of luck with your music!
2. *ping* My mind pinged going *What is this Christian EDM?* Every couple of weeks, for about a year now...in the endless quest to get back up to speed (I walked away from the commercial music industry a decade ago) my radar will pick up inside information, promotional strategies and music treasures that blow me away or the *ping* moments that deserve further attention and examination. Like this...New Artist... Vita Aternus? Aternus. Ate..rnuns... Anyways... The mind-frame right off the bat is defeat and hopelessness. Understandable. You're new to the game with passion for the genre and you have a sense of purpose. Motivated by other factors beyond the beat internally...with added value to offer externally... A little faith in mix baby! So...The intention has been defined. This is good. You have moved up to "New Artist with a Clue" let's continue....shall we? I have questions...So... let me get this straight. Spotify doesn't recognize this sub-genre? Christian EDM is not official? What the? You mean to tell me... in this current popular music marketplace. A place with crammed shelf space in every aisle, jam packed inventory corner to corner in storage behind the receiving area with semi-truck trailers in the parking lot stuffed with product and several more trucks parked off to the side of the road because the parking lot is full, including a convoy in route on I-80 that needs to drop cargo. While down in the basement pouring through the pipelines called "new music" not by the thousands, because thousands would be inventory overflow and somewhat manageable. We have One Hundreds of Thousands songs going live and available daily, elbowing and clawing to get upstairs on the shelf. A supply and demand dynamic with an obvious oversupply of submissions that makes the term "flooded marketplace" completely inaccurate, which adds new creative measurements like "Biblical Levels of Flooded" or "Flooded X 2 to the 2nd Power Flooded" or "Flooded beyond what is flooded with more floods coming in tomorrow flooded." ...And there is a open space? With an audience? Dude...this is opportunity! You've just identified the target. And the lack of targets more importantly. The question is...how to get in? But, the better question is "What steps do I need to take for complete domination of this sector?" Top of list...That name is...*shaking head back and forth* I hope and pray your music is better than the stage name. For real. Come'n man. All the cool names involved in the Catholic/Protestant church split. Then the splits in the Protestant splits. Preachers from the past that lead movements or the name of the movement itself. Names that role off your tongue and sound sweet AND other people can read and repeat out loud. Some dude in the history books will align better in deed and in syllables...because Vita At can't pronounce doesn't resonate... Word to the wise. Don't mention Ai. And what do you need Ai for in EDM? Ai generated EDM drums? Wow.There's endless sources of beats to pick off and countless websites that will let you download free sample packsin 2026. You don't need Ai in production. Ai for Post-Production only. Trust me on this. Next...the blocks of time you spend studying the bible for that snappy quote out loud chapter and verse to prove your point? Put a fork in it. Time better spent on research and development. You'll find out what life means later. Right now it's shovel and dig. Where do the top DJ's hang out on-line? The soundcloud pages of the producers in the genre? What about YouTube? Find the channels, subscribe and comment. Reddit will help in research and the refinement of "Operation Domination" and should serve as a hub to read, respond and gain knowledge. Pay attention to areas that need to be serviced. Find the holes and see if you can fill them up as you take notes. The mind-frame here is not "how can I get in" but more "what can I do to help" -- Apply these actions for about a year. Then get a hold me. With a better DJ name...Peace from Tack-Fu of the 85 decibel Monks

review_text:
Title: My daily mixes just suck and there is no way to dislike

PlanPremiumCountryNLDeviceAllOperating SystemAll My Question or Issue My daily mixes suck so bad and they seem to get worse every day I have no idea how Spotify decides to choose these songs for me and there is no way to tell I dislike these songs

--- Community replies ---
1. Hey @Armand, Thanks for reaching out to the Community. Keep in mind that the entries in your Daily Mixes are largely based on your personal likes, but also on your region and the music libraries of users with similar tastes. That's why it might take a while for the list to get filled with more songs that haven't come up on your radar yet. Note that on mobile devices you can like/hide the tracks there. You can help the algorithm by reworking your library with new liked songs and albums and exploring new genres or sets of artists. This should give the system a boost and will often mean that a couple of weeks later, you'll start getting more diverse Daily Mixes! Hope this info is useful. Let us know if there's anything else we can help with. Mihail ModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!
2. The more songs I like the worse the selection seems to get and the button to dislike songs seems to have disappeared for months now, when will spotify fix that bug?
3. Hey @Armand, Thanks for keeping in touch. You can still ''dislike'' songs from your Daily Mixes by tapping on Hide this song, either on the Now Playing view, or by tapping on the 3-dot context menu next to the song's title. This option is only available in the mobile app. Remember that the songs added to your Daily Mixes are based on your listening activity. We also suggest checking this article for tips on how to improve the playlists made for you. If you need a hand with anything else, we're always a post away. Take care! CarlosEModerator Help others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate my answer, maybe give me a Like.

Hello,I noticed what appears to be a metadata/discography issue on the Queensrÿche artist page in Germany.Several studio albums are available on Spotify and their tracks can be played, but the albums themselves do not appear in the artist's album discography.Examples:Rage for Order (1986)Promised Land (1994)Hear in the Now Frontier (1997)Other albums such as The Warning and Empire are displayed correctly.The missing albums can still be found through direct links or individual tracks, which suggests that the recordings are available in Spotify's catalog but are not being displayed correctly in the artist's album list.I have reproduced this on multiple devices, so this does not appear to be a local cache issue.Could Spotify please review the album-to-artist mapping and metadata for these releases and restore them to the Queensrÿche discography if appropriate?Artist page: Queensrÿche on Spotify⁠�Thank you.

Keep in mind that the way the content is displayed in the app (including its metadata), is the way that it was submitted by the artist or the label/distributor. Nevertheless, if you notice inconsistencies, you can still report them by following the steps in the article mentioned above. If anything else comes up, the Community will be here for you! Title: Songs are grayed out in my playlist but are still available on the platform.

PlanPremiumCountryUSADeviceiPhone, Mac, and my PC My Question or IssueI have a very long playlist (1,338 songs) and recently noticed a handful of songs are grayed out. Some of them have actually been removed from the platform, but the majority are still available for streaming. If I go to the artist's profile, I can play the song from there just not from my playlist. I checked and there are no filters or restrictions that should prevent the songs from playing. For example, both Wet and Nice Nice by Dazey and the Scouts are grayed out on my playlist but are still available on Spotify. I can play both songs when I go directly to Dazey and the Scouts' artist profile. If I manually go through each song, I can add them back to my playlist but since this playlist is SO long, I'd prefer not to. I also like to keep the songs in the order I discovered them as a personal note. What can I do to get the songs back on my playlist?

--- Community replies ---
1. Hi there, @Rasiadunn Thanks for reaching out! Songs could be grayed out on your playlist, yet available elsewhere, for different reasons. For instance, due to Local Files syncing errors. Just to confirm, have you imported the grayed out songs as Local Files from your device or you saved them directly from Spotify? Another reason could be regional licensing restrictions, or a song's version mismatch (the version in your playlist is no longer available on Spotify, but a newer or different one exists). Try logging out from your account then logging in twice to solve the syncing matter. Also, clearing cache and updating the app might help. Restarting your device may solve connectivity issues causing the glitch too. Finally, try removing/re-adding a specific grayed out song to the playlist to check if it's a song's new version type of issue. Hope this helps. Let us know how it goes! Paula_ViModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!

The more you play your favourite music, add it to your Liked Songs and playlists and overall engage with it, the better the recommendations will adapt to you. If you however notice consistent and significant discrepancies in the recommendations you get, please let us know as that may be something worth investigating. Hope this clears things up. Hi there @esainburg, Thank you for your reply in this thread It's currently not possible to mark all songs at the same time in the web player or the mobile app. However, it should work if you're doing it in the desktop app. When you use Ctrl + A in Windows devices (in Apple devices press Command ⌘ + A) on the Spotify desktop app, all your liked songs would be selected (even if it's more than 1000 songs!). review_text:
Title: Playlists not showing up even when set to "Public I have a premium account and I just started having this issue... (I'm using the desktop version of Spotify on my MacBook Air)I make a playlist, add songs and a cover if needed then set it to playlist. But, it doesn't show up on my profile and other people can't see it too. I tried this with my other account and I had the same problem too. I also tried setting the playlist public on my phone and it shows up there but not on my desktop version. What's going on and how can I solve this? I'm someone who loves making playlists for others so this is quite annoying Update: I tried doing it on the Web player and it worked but it still didn't show up on my desktop version. --- Community replies --- 1. Welcome to the Spotify Community, folks! Click on each section below for more details. Friends can't see the playlist you just shared with them? Spoiler (Highlight to read) It's possible that the playlist was reported and the system detected something unusual about it, so it made it visible only to you. I'd recommend checking your inbox for any recent emails from Spotify you may have received as there may be something in there which could give a bit more insight on the matter. If the issue persists, it's worth making a new playlist and copying the contents of the old one into it (you can do so from the three dots menu of the old playlist > Add to other playlist > New playlist). After that, you can add it to your profile and it should show up.
It's possible that the playlist was reported and the system detected something unusual about it, so it made it visible only to you. I'd recommend checking your inbox for any recent emails from Spotify you may have received as there may be something in there which could give a bit more insight on the matter.   If the issue persists, it's worth making a new playlist and copying the contents of the old one into it (you can do so from the three dots menu of the old playlist > Add to other playlist > New playlist). After that, you can add it to your profile and it should show up.
Playlists not showing up on your profile? Spoiler (Highlight to read) Keep in mind that there's a limit of 200 playlists that can be shown on your profile so if you have more, that might be the reason not all of them are showing. Also, the public playlists on your profile are displayed in the Custom order you've set in the desktop app / Web Player. Any changes to the order of your public playlists will be reflected on your profile as well: In Your Library, set the filter to Playlists. Set the sort order to Custom order and rearrange your playlists as you see fit.
Keep in mind that there's a limit of 200 playlists that can be shown on your profile so if you have more, that might be the reason not all of them are showing.   Also, the public playlists on your profile are displayed in the Custom order you've set in the desktop app / Web Player. Any changes to the order of your public playlists will be reflected on your profile as well: In Your Library, set the filter to Playlists. Set the sort order to Custom order and rearrange your playlists as you see fit.
Playlists not showing up in Search? Spoiler (Highlight to read)In case your playlist isn't showing up in Search, this is because not all search results are indexed. There are multiple factors that come to play in order for a playlist to show up in Search such as popularity, number of followers, uniqueness of name, etc. This post goes more in depth, so feel free to check it out for more info on the matter. For the time being, I recommend sharing the link directly with your friends.

Hope this clears things up. The Community is always here if there's anything else you need assistance with 2. Hey folks, Thanks for posting in the thread. It's worth mentioning that here the team is currently investigating the issue that public playlists are not visible on profiles and there's other users that are experiencing this. You can check out the ongoing issues thread, add your details there (as well as your vote) and subscribe to the thread in order not to miss any updates on the matter. Appreciate your cooperation! In the meantime, we're here if there's anything else we can assist you with. 3. Hey everyone, Thanks for reaching out on the Community! Keep in mind that there are 2 different ways to set your playlist visibility - by making playlists public or private, and by managing public playlists published to your Spotify profile. You can change the privacy settings for your playlist at any time by clicking the 3 dots > Make private/Make public. When you make a playlist private, nobody can access it, even if they have a link to it or search for it by name. For the playlists to also be visible to other users who visits your profile, you'll also need to add it to your profile. You can do so by clicking the 3 dots > Add to profile. Note that only public playlists can be published to your Spotify profile. If you've made sure that all those setting are correct and you still have this issue, we recommend clearing stored cache on your device(s), and signing out from the app & back in again once it's done. Completing these steps should resync you account info. We're just a post away if there's anything else you need assistance with. Title: What happened to the ability to sort music in a playlist by artist or song?

I used to be able to sort my playlist by artist, song, album, etc. Is that feature going to return? I found that this was the only way to break the song-playing algorithm, which sometimes gets stuck playing the same songs over and over.

review_text:
Title: shuffle playlist seems to play the same songs

Whenever i'm in the car, i turn on android auto and play a playlist that's already on shuffle, but on the way home i hear the same songs. I have 809 songs in the playlist, i don't think in a 15 minute drive i should be hearing the same sequence of songs.

--- Community replies ---
1. Hey folks! Some of you have reported that only a specific bracket of your playlists is being repeated. While the expected behaviour here is that Shuffle is completely random and shouldn't prioritize any songs, we've noticed your feedback and we're eager to look into this for you, so we'd like to ask you for the following details: The URI of an affected playlist. How many songs it contains and roughly how many of them get played & repeated? Does this happen with only specific playlists or all of them? The device this occurs on & its OS version. Your Spotify app version. The time & date this has occurred on so that we can check your activity backstage. Tip: Note that clearing your cached data has been reported to reset this behavior. Cheers! We'll be on the lookout for new replies here @SirRoxas @KillerFangz @Scottishvillain @clownfragment
2. Hi folks, also worth noting that the shuffle behaviour changed fairly recently so @KillerFangz when you shuffle a playlist, and then click another track in the playlist or queue, it will play that track, but preserve the original shuffle sequence, so will continue with that sequence. To get a fresh shuffle, you need to toggle the shuffle button on/off. You can also find the latest details on how Shuffle works now in these posts: https://newsroom.spotify.com/2025-11-13/shuffle-update-fewer-repeats https://engineering.atspotify.com/2025/11/shuffle-making-random-feel-more-human If you are experiencing repetition when toggling the shuffle button on/off, then please provide the details as @Vasil noted above.
3. PlanFree/PremiumCountry Device(iPhone 8, Samsung Galaxy 9, Macbook Pro late 2016)Operating System(iOS 10, Android Oreo, Windows 10,etc.) My Question or IssueI have close to 3000 songs in my liked songs and shuffle it as my main playlist as I have for years. Recently I've noticed that I'm hearing the same 50-100 songs multiple times a day and would love if the algorithm that chooses these songs could stop. While in the gym today I heard the same song twice in less than an hour. I tried to attach the screen recording but it wouldn't allow me to.
4. I have a premium family account and listen to all my 'Liked' songs on shuffle across multiple devices - work laptop, home pc, home stereo (via connect) and phone - and regularly switch which device is playing. I also regularly use each of the different devices to control each other, depending on which device I am listening from vs. which I am using at the time - e.g., in office with work laptop playing, but using my personal PC at the time. Or sitting in the lounge listening on my stereo, but controlling via phone.Recently (last two weeks), I have noticed my shuffle function no longer seems to work properly at all. I get the same pseudo-random songs, in the same order, over and over again. I have noticed this is related to switching devices - at first I thought it was just that each device remembered where it was, and so as I switched play between the devices I was just getting duplicate runs - but after two weeks of the same songs over and over, in the same pseudo-random order, I am more inclined to think this is a bug. (Yes, I like 'Charlie Peacock - Dear Friend, but not multiple times per day, when I have another 7,000 songs I also want to listen to!). It seems like all devices are remembering where the previous device started, and because I switch often (I do the school run and WFH), they are all stuck with the same location/seed?How can I get better random performance? At a guess, it looks like the random function is account-bound (I'm guessing it happens server-side). I want a true random - not this pseudo-random garbage.

analysis.pain_point: Smart Shuffle forces the same popular songs
analysis.user_need: A more random or customized shuffle feature.
analysis.summary: Critique of Smart Shuffle for playing the same popular songs.

review_text:
Title: Playlist drag-and-drop only moves songs to top or bottom

PlanDuo/Premium CountryNorway DeviceSamsung GalaxyOperating SystemAndroid My Question or IssueIn playlist edit mode on the mobile app, I can no longer freely drag songs to a specific position in the playlist. When I try to move a song, it only allowed me to put it on the very top or the very bottom instead of being placed between other songs.The playlist is set to “Custom order,” and I am the owner/editor of the playlist. Restarting the app and changing sort order did not fix the issue. 

--- Community replies ---
1. Hey folks, Thanks to some excellent investigative work by our colleague @Yordan, we were able to identify the root cause of this issue. It appears that the behavior occurs when the drag and drop handle is long pressed. That triggers a feature where you can select multiple songs which then only lets you move them to the top or bottom of the playlist. To get the usual behavior just quickly press and drag instead of holding it down. @Yordan was also kind enough to share a screen recording demonstrating the correct action. We hope this helps! MihailYModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself! View solution in original post
2. Hi there, @PrivateUsername29 Thanks for reaching out and welcome to the Spotify Community! If you're still experiencing this issue, to investigate further, send us the following details: The affected device (make/model) and its OS version. The exact Spotify app version you’re currently running. A short video recording of the issue in action. You can attach it to your next response by using the Insert Video option in the post editor or upload it to YouTube/Google Drive and share the link with us (make sure the video has the permissions for anyone to watch it). We'll be on the lookout for your reply! Paula_ViModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!
3. Same problem. It just started a few days ago. Spotify must have changed something last uodate. .Paula Vi reply not helpful as it is a spotify issue, not caused by user
4. What Paula is asking for is standard information to be able to understand the root cause of the issue. She isn't inferring that it's user error.FYI, I am also experiencing the issue on my Google Pixel 9a running Android 16. Spotify version is 9.1.44.2120

review_text:
Title: Not able to play all liked songs from an artist

On the desktop app, there used to be a very useful feature on the artist page where I could access and play all the songs I had liked from that specific artist in one continuous list. This made it easy to revisit my favorite tracks across different albums without having to manually open each album one by one. Recently, this functionality changed. Now, the “Liked Songs” section on the artist page only shows the albums I’ve liked, and I can only play them individually. This removes a lot of convenience and makes the listening experience more fragmented and time-consuming. I’d like to suggest bringing back the previous behavior, allowing users to play all liked songs from an artist in a single, unified list again. It was a simple but extremely valuable feature, especially for users who like to explore artists deeply.

--- Community replies ---
1. Further to this idea (which is really correcting a horrendous error in the change Spotify made to this section). For many artists ,I like lots of releases. eg Prince - 33 releases. However, I can never see more than 10 now, and they are all listed across the top of the section. Trying to use library filtering doesn't work as Prince appears in many titles. And of course, I still can't just play the songs/releases I've liked for this artist
2. This should also be added to the android app. It used to be possible and its been a request for years to bring this back.
3. Please bring this back! I’ll gladly re-subscribe if I can filter artists albums that I’ve added to my liked list. I want a PERSONALIZED music listening experience without having to make new playlists all the time
4. Hi folks, Thanks for posting about this, we really appreciate your input! At Spotify, we’re always looking for opportunities to enhance the listening experience for both fans and creators. We routinely conduct a number of tests, and some of them may ultimately end up paving the way for our broader user experience. We'd suggest liking the songs within an album, not the album itself, so that they show as a list of liked songs on the artist page. We understand that can be more time-consuming, but it's a workaround for the issue you've described. @x202e, this is still available in the mobile app if you go to the artist page and the above applies, so if you're not seeing anything, can you send us a couple of screenshots? We'd be happy to check further. The Community is always here if something else comes up. DessiModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a "Like".Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!

Title: Restore the function of sorting songs within a playlists

I formally request that the classic drag and drop functionality be restored for free-form reordering of songs within playlists in the mobile app. In recent updates, the process has become less intuitive: selecting the song is now required, and the system only offers the rigid options of moving it to the "top" or "bottom" of the list. This eliminates the flexibility of quickly positioning a theme in a specific intermediate location, negatively affecting the user experience.

--- Community replies ---
1. Hi there @ItzLuis82, Thanks for reaching out. We've moved your post here where it better fits. It's still possible to drag and drop songs around and re-order them however you'd like without any restrictions on mobile. See this article for the full info on that and keep in mind that some features, as mentioned there, are for Premium only. Additionally, when the drag and drop handle is long pressed, that triggers a feature where you can select multiple songs which then only lets you move them to the top or bottom of the playlist. This can lock you out of drag and dropping freely, so check if this is the case for you. See this thread for further info. If this isn't what you see, could you let us know the exact behaviour you're facing? If this is some kind of issue, we'll be happy to help you get everything working as it should once again. Keep us posted. JoanModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!
2. Thank you for assisting me. In the same way, the same thing continues to happen on cell phones, I ask please that it be as before, I create several large playlists!
3. Hey again @ItzLuis82, When you're in this exact edit mode, tap and drag the 3-line button on the right to move a single song. Tapping and holding down on the song itself enables multiple selection instead I believe you should be good to go after this, but if there's any questions, don't hesitate to reach back VasilModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!
4. Thanks for your response. Now I did it and by tapping the three line button 3 times I was able to move the song anywhere. In the same way, before it was more dynamic, with just one touch, regardless of whether you played the song or the three lines, you could drag with your fingers and move the songs anywhere. I have the following idea that I would like you to analyze for the next update. Being able to listen to a part of the songs in that editing function seems great to me that they have put it in, I hope they keep it that way. But to move a song in a preferred place (not at the beginning or end) I suggest that it can go back to how it was before, with this I think about the following: keep the selection, but instead of being able to move to the beginning or end and delete the selected song you can only delete it, and cancel the option to move to the beginning or end and it can be dragged with your fingers without having to touch 3 times, before it was more dynamic, faster and simpler, if it weren't for your answer I wouldn't know, that's why I suggest this, I hope for your return, thank you!

Title: AI "verified artists" with "About" never saying it's AI

Recently had Lyra Vesperi in my Discover Weekly, which I immediately knew was AI from the art. But how much of the music Spotify is pushing is AI and harder to catch? Why do they allow it and not make it it's own genre or at least tag it with a 'Generative AI' flag?I don't like the truth that Spotify is sneaking AI 'music' into it's recommendations. Time spent listening to the slop is listening time where Spotify doesn't have to pay a real artist. How can I trust any 'artist' on Spotify now? Am I forced to google every artist I hear to make sure Spotify isn't trying to trick me?For all that's holy, Please remove all AI content, or give listeners the option to 'opt-in' to AI music. Nobody wants to listen to AI music. It's not art, and it's not music.

--- Community replies ---
1. How hard ca it be to give AI generated music its own genre, or at least a tag to distinguish it from the music made by real life artists? The soon er the better
2. I have a friend who makes AI music, but she’s a lyricist with no singing or melody talent…I don’t think she’s on any platform, but writing melody and learning and performing a song seem more time consuming than writing the lyrics, either way I’m sure there are “artists” out there doing all three using AI, just tell it I want a pop song about xyz sung by a female..I think at least one aspect minimum be required to be done by a human to be considered Art
3. I have the opposite problem where I can sing but have no talent to write music or lyrics, I’ve collaborated with her to sing a song that she wrote the lyrics to and AI provided the melody in the style she requested
4. @johoxtra I agree with you. I think there needs to be a specific genre for AI music. As a musican, I don't think it's harmful to have AI music (to some extent), because it creates a new genre. But at the same time it can be harmful because they're able to mass produce music, meanwhile real musicians spend a lot of time working on their music. I feel like there should be rules that you have to disclose that your music was made with ai, and if it was then maybe limiting the amount of time you can upload music

analysis.user_need: Truly fresh and obscure music recommendations in weekly discovery.
analysis.summary: Frustrated that Discover Weekly plays the same artists repeatedly.

review_text:
Title: AI Artist / Content Notification

I asked an AI app about my concern that AI artists and material are not being flagged by Spotify prior to their release. It told me to write the following. You can use the chat/contact options and state that:You want clear labeling of fully AI-generated music.You want AI-generated artists identified as such.You want the ability to filter AI-generated content out of recommendations and playlists.You believe listeners should be informed whether they are hearing human performers, AI-generated performers, or a mixture of both.I believe AI has its place (example above) but should be required to make it known when it has "created" material. I believe that Spotify should be leading the promotion of music and video artists, not undermining their value.

--- Community replies ---
1. Totally agree and was just about to post the same topic on here. I have recently found some new artists who I have started to listen to and then have found out they are fully AI generated. AI generated images of the people, AI generated voices and music. What put me over the top is that one of those "artists" released a "live" album which I listened to and it sounded off and then I found out they were fully AI generated. I also would like to know upfront if these artists are AI generated either fully or partially and have the option to toggle off AI generated music if I want to listen to actual humans. I'd hope that Spotify would take the lead here and have heard of others switching over to Apple music to avoid the AI artists on Spotify.
2. Hey, There are a couple of active ideas in the Community that you can vote for which proposes the improvements that you mention here, here are the links:Mark / Disable AI Generated SongsAi generated content should be labeled In the meantime, there is an article published on the Spotify Newsroom website which explains what measures Spotify have taken with regard to AI on their platform. You can read it here: https://newsroom.spotify.com/2025-09-25/spotify-strengthens-ai-protections/. In particular, you might be interested in the April 2026 update. Hope that helps

review_text:
Title: What is the one song you listen on repeat?

For me, it has to be ionwannagoback by funeral. I am a biggg person when it comes to glitchcore and hyperpop type songs after discovering it around 2024 (i cant even remember lol) ^^

--- Community replies ---
1. Nice question! I use the "repeat one" feature relatively frequently, and there isn't just one song that I enjoy listening to on repeat, but many such tracks that vary depending on the day. I'd say that the most frequent types of occasions when I'll listen to a song on repeat is when the song is more unfamiliar, such as when I've just discovered it or I'm re-listening after a long time. The motivation behind listening to a song on repeat is usually to ensure that after many listens, a part of the track doesn't become boring, tedious, annoying, or otherwise cause me to decide to listen to a different song instead. If after many listens, the song still seems to be a positive listening experience from beginning to end, I'll add it to relevant playlists. After all, the purpose of playlists (at least for me, anyway) is to have good songs saved in one place for re-listening, and I try to avoid adding songs that I think I might possible want to remove shortly thereafter (although this still happens sometimes). For instance, right now I'm revisiting this song by Clouds Taste Satanic by listening on repeat: spotify:track:5p8Yb05hH5ZZ5tHvEllx37:small At the moment, I think that "One Third of the Sun" sounds quite nice from start to end, but I also think I'll continue listening on repeat for a while longer, so that I can feel more confident about adding it to relevant playlists. Cheers!

analysis.user_need: Greater variety and freshness in music suggestions.
analysis.summary: Complains about recommendations being highly repetitive and loops of the same songs.

review_text:
Title: DJ feature feedback

I sent this over to spotify on twitter as feedback and i am posting it here to see what others think. "okay i will start by saying i love the idea. i have been using it quite a bit and always get excited to hear what songs are chosen. but there are a few things i would like to point out for starters, i find myself skipping a lot of the tracks or hitting the reshuffle. I listen to a lot of different genres and sometimes will play a playlist for pop punk or 2010s pop depending on what i am doing. but when i use the DJ, i get a lot of pop punk and normally dont want to listen to it. so i skip it until I go back to the EDM songs that i would like to hear. the second thing is the feature is quite glitchy. when I listen to the DJ, if i play another song or playlist and go back to the DJ, it will start from the beginning of the DJ instead of picking up where i left off. this is my biggest complaint about the feature by far. I was also listening to the DJ yesterday and ended up going through all of the music so it would only loop back from the beginning instead of continuing to play other songs. one last thing is mostly for EDM. sometimes the genres that are chosen are not represented well. one example is the first song was from quiet bison, who does future bass, and then it started playing kayzo next after that song, which is heavy dubstep. this is mostly just a nit pick but is still something i would like to point out. my ideas to fix these issues start with the first complaint. being able to choose what genres you want to listen to in the DJ would go a long way, or maybe asking which artists you would prefer to hear. cause most days i would only want to listen to EDM genres and i will get pop punk, punk, kpop and metal all back to back (even when i only listen to those genres on occasion) the second fix i would put forward is having the DJ continuously choose songs instead of having a set list and nothing more. it seems like that sort of technology is already in spotify since it is in things like enhanced playlists and also recommended songs for playlists. lastly the fix for having the DJ start from the beginning should be addressed. if only one thing is taken from all of this, that should be the first priority."

--- Community replies ---
1. Just wanted to hop in here and say I love the DJ feature but concur that the sampling tends to loop a lot. As a user, I expect to receive a different set each time I click the DJ button. Love this feature though! Great work Spotify.
2. I have been waiting for the DJ feature to become public since a beta tester posted a snippet of how it worked on tik tok months ago. My one complaint is that most of the song recommendations it gives me are already on one of my playlists! Kinda disappointing but I agree with OP that they should change up the recommendations and not have a set list to play from
3. Spotify DJ? Nope. 1. The accent is annoying. Cool guy from NY (?) doesn't suit me at all.2. The music selection: meh. Like the Made For selections MUCH better3. Stop talking! I paid extra for no commercials, I really don't want to listen to AI yammer PR stuff at me. Over all, hard no.
4. About the changing songs then going back to the DJ, have you tried "adding to queue" instead of just playing the other track then going back? That way the song gets added into the current queue created by the DJ instead of leaving it for the other track then starting it up again. Edit: I think the next track after playing the added to queue track was correct, but then it went back to playing the same metal tracks it played earlier when I started listening to the DJ, so maybe this isn't a solution. The DJ seems to be a work in progress atm, and pretty disappointing since I seem to prefer other generated playlists or just playing from my likes.

analysis.summary: Critique of Smart Shuffle for playing the same popular songs.

Title: Wrapped 2024

Hey! Thanks for coming to the Spotify Community.We’re excited to hear about your 2024 Wrapped experience. Please use this thread to let us know how you're finding your 2024 Wrapped, share your feedback, and share any issues you may be experiencing. You can find this year's Wrapped by tapping Home and selecting the Wrapped feed. This link should also take you to the experience when you open it on your mobile device. If you still don’t see it, please try the steps below: Log out and log back in. Clear the app's cache. Restart your device. Reinstall the app. Try a different mobile device. We’ll be keeping an eye on this thread and reporting back to the right folks, as well as linking to any known issues which may arise. Please note: Wrapped may not be available to all users, as it’s subject to specific eligibility criteria. Hiya fam, To remove a podcast from your homepage on Spotify, you can try the following steps:Go to the "Podcasts" section of your Spotify app.Find the podcast that you want to remove from your homepage.Tap and hold on the podcast's icon or cover art until a menu appears.Select "Remove from Home" from the menu.The podcast should now be removed from your homepage.If the podcast still appears on your homepage after following these steps, try closing and reopening the Spotify app or logging out and back in to see if the changes have taken effect.I hope this helps, -Prague the Dog

But when your AI makes a bad suggestion, it stays within the genre of the song you didn't like when you try forwarding to another song. The algorithm is uses to suggest songs is way off. Please bring back the Discover Weekly. That one was much more often on point. It turned me on to do much good music. Please bring back Discover Weekly.

review_text:
Title: Outdated Artist Profile Picture (Inaccurate Metadata - Artist Reunited)

PlanPremium DuoCountryIndonesiaDeviceAndroid 11Operating SystemWindows 11 Hi Spotify Community Team,I would like to report an issue regarding an outdated and inaccurate artist profile picture. Artist Name: SCREWArtist Spotify URL: https://open.spotify.com/artist/7MSItZfamOkuh4YY4imHRa?si=rEEMMlg6T0muuBOq98_PdQ Issue Description:The current profile picture for this artist is extremely outdated (more than 10 years old). In fact, it is not even a real photo of the artist, but rather an old album cover illustration from over a decade ago.The artist has recently reunited and made a official comeback, and they now have new, updated official photos.However, their Spotify profile has not been updated to reflect this new era, which creates confusion for listeners and new fans. I am having a hard time reaching their management because all of their official social media accounts (X and Instagram) have turned off the reply and direct message features. I have even tried reaching out directly to two of the band members. They said they would forward it to their management, but there has been no progress for weeks. Could the Spotify team or content moderators please look into this metadata/profile issue or help flag this to the relevant content team?Thank you so much for your help!

review_text:
Title: How can you report fake AI artists?

I found this AI "artists": https://open.spotify.com/artist/5iDxkYqkajjBahsudnNB0H?si=Bhx0Q_j0RhKnw_xPR-aMKQ that hijacks the recommendations by listing popular artists as performers on their songs. The music is obviously AI garbage. Is there a way report that? 

--- Community replies ---
1. On another post on the same topic someone directed to a feature request for a "dissable AI music" tickbox or something. Go there and vote, seems like its the only thing we can do right now. Link here: https://community.spotify.com/t5/Live-Ideas/Mark-Disable-AI-Generated-Songs/idi-p/6641329
2. Hey, folks, Your feedback on this is important, as it may ultimately end up paving the way for our broader user experience. Thanks for sharing it here - we appreciate it. We're working on introducing clearer AI disclosures in the song credits, as well as stricter impersonation rules. These updates will help protect artists’ identities, ensure royalties are distributed fairly, and give you - the listeners - more transparency. Note that AI disclosures do not affect how the music is treated for recommendations, but you can still manually block any artist or hide/snooze any song you don't want the app playing from the three dots menu. For an in-depth look I'd recommend checking out this article. The Community is here if there's anything you need assistance with. YordanModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution". What are Solutions exactly?If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a "Like".Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself! View solution in original post
3. Hi @jnnx You can report by pressing on three dots near the artist profile -- scroll down press on option report to report from the listed reason Hope this helps TanyaSSpotify StarHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate my answer, maybe give me a Like.Note: I'm not a Spotify employee.
4. Hey, I came across a spotify artist called "Gazela" that is just straight up stealing music from Kesha, along with her imagery (both by taking the images or making AI mockups of them). The actual stolen songs have the same lyrics and a bunch of similarities to Kesha's ones, but with a different sound, and Kesha is not credited anywhere.I tried to report this artist but there's no option to select the music as the thing you want to report, so I wanted to know if there was some way I could report this?

review_text:
Title: Collaborative Playlists Still Not Working

PlanPremium CountryNetherlands My Question or IssueI saw that the issue with collaborative playlists not being editable by collaborators is marked as solved, but it still does not work for me. I've shared the playlist using the "Invite collaborators" link, had the other user add the playlist to their profile with the heart icon, and they are still unable to add or remove songs. 

--- Community replies ---
1. Hi @afrazo, It's possible that they're opening the playlist in the Web Player, which doesn't support collaborative playlists yet. Could you ask them to copy the invite link and paste it in the desktop/mobile app's search bar? That should do the trick. You can also generate a new invite link, if you want to. If the issue persists, ask them to first remove the playlist from their library and then repeat the aforementioned process. Cheers YordanModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution". What are Solutions exactly?If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a "Like".Are you new to the Community? Some screenshots of the behaviour would be very helpful. Keep us posted. JoanModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!

review_text:
Title: Skeevy audiobook keeps appearing at the top of my recommendations

Repeatedly getting this disgusting "**bleep** Guide For Men" put right at the very top of my audiobook recommendations. I don't listen to audiobooks and I'm not a man so I'm curious if there's some kind of way that I can block this one particular audiobook (without listening-I track my listening habits with a 3rd party app and do not want this showing up there) or all audiobooks altogether. Probably a ridiculous request, but I figure I'll ask anyways. I primarily use the desktop version of Spotify. Also, I've reported the audiobook.

--- Community replies ---
1. Hey @user-removed, Thank you for reaching out to the Community. We appreciate your feedback regarding this. At the moment, we don't have a way to block or delete an audiobook from your home page. As an option, we understand you don't really listen to audiobooks, but it's worth listening to different Audiobooks content so it changes the recommendations over time. Also, a couple of similar ideas have been posted in our Ideas exchange. We'd also recommend showing your support for them by adding your vote (using the thumbs-up button) to the following ideas: BE ABLE TO DELETE AUDIOBOOKS [All Platforms] Customize Start Screen Hope this helps. If anything else comes up, the Community will be here for you. OscarDCModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!
2. I have played a few audiobooks in an attempt to change it and it has affected everything else slightly but a different audiobook by the same author (I changed my account's gender to female to try to get rid of these but it turns out she's made one for both genders) still remains at the very top of my recommendations. I've marked it as not interested in the audiobooks tab so I don't understand why this is so persistent.
3. The funny thing is that I have a book by the same author, but for couples, as my top recommended audiobook. And an even worse one as the third recommendation and more of the same as fifth. I'm pretty sure they're just sponsored books so those of us paying spotify for premium accounts are being subjected to things we don't want because someone else is paying them to do it. Great.I've been searching for an answer to it and with the latest version of the desktop spotify app there are buttons at the very top of the recommended section for "All", "Music", "Podcasts", and "Audiobooks". Click on Music and the audiobook section goes away. The ironic thing is that I've actually redeemed a few purchased audiobooks on spotify when I could have redeemed them elsewhere because I was trying to support a competitor to audible. And now spotify's only solution to displaying inappropriate content on my work computer is to just completely hide podcasts/audiobooks that they're trying to break into. Dumb decision IMO.Anyway, hope that helps.
4. Same issue. I did nothing to generate this recommendation and Spotify offers zero tools to address it. Despite being 100% about sleazy **bleep** stories the book is not even listed as explicit, so it can't be removed with a filter. I reported it twice with no action. Come on Spotify. Get this junk out of here.

review_text:
Title: My Wrapped content is inaccurate

My Spotify wrapped in incorrect and incomplete, a song i listen to daily is at the bottom and has only been played 25 times supposedly. It seems it has only tracked the last 2/3 months? Also it's only 5 hours long even though I listened 19days, my boyfriend listened 7 days and has almost 7 hours on his wrapped. 

--- Community replies ---
1. Hey there @emmaniekerk, Thanks for posting in the Community! There are a couple of things you can take into consideration. Your Wrapped is based on your 2025 listening activity. If perhaps you share your account with someone else (e.g. while using a smart speaker), or if you tend to leave your account playing content in the background, keep in mind this activity affects the Wrapped. You can also check for any unrecognized apps on your account page that are connected to your account. If you notice anything suspicious you can remove it with the REMOVE ACCESS button. You can easily reconnect the apps that you use whenever you want. You can follow these steps to secure your account if needed. It is important to mention that offline listening counts towards your top songs and total minutes listened; Private Session and Exclude from your taste profile count only towards your total minutes listened. Unfortunately, we’re unable to update your Wrapped data or playlists. Make sure to check out the following article for more info on how your Wrapped data is being calculated - Surprised by Your 2025 Wrapped? Here’s a Look at How the Data Comes to Life. We hope you found this helpful. We're always here to help.

Title: Recently played artists not updating

The Recently Played Artists feature on profiles that have it turned on has not been updating in over 24 hours. That thread has been closed and marked as resolved as this feature has been functioning like normal for several months.

1. Hey folks, Thanks for your replies and all the info! Since a lot of troubleshooting steps have been exhausted in the thread already, can you check the following: You're playing the track all the way through to ensure the artist will appear in Recently played artists and the song in Recents The playlist you're listening to isn't marked as “Exclude from your taste profile” from the three dots within the playlist You’re not in a Private session or listening in Offline mode If the issue keeps happening afterwards, try toggling "Recently played artists" off/on again to force a resync and make sure your app is always up-to-date. We'll keep monitoring the thread, but for the time being we aren't able to reproduce the issue and as it seems to be happening sporadically on your end, it might be isolated to your current setup. If possible, try using another network and make sure Spotify isn't being restricted by your firewall settings. The Community is here if you have any other questions or concerns.

review_text:
Title: Issues adding a Smart Shuffle suggestion to the current playlist

First my apologies if I selected the wrong help-section, but I could not find one for playlists and since my spotify app runs on a windows desktop this was the closest one I could find.The issue: For my playlists I often have Smart Shuffle turned on to see what Spotify comes up with. If I like a suggestion I add it to the playlist.But the Spotify app than adds it also again to the top of the play-queue, where the song is already playing.Is there a way to turn that automatically adding an added song to the play-queue off? Since in the case of an already playing song I don't "need" it again in my play-queue.Thank you in advance.Nemelis Plan Premium Country Netherlands Device PC Operating System Windows 10 My Question or Issue

--- Community replies ---
1. Hi @Nemelis, Thanks for posting this thread in the Community! As far as I understand, adding an already playing Smart Shuffle suggested song to a playlist, it gets 'duplicated' in the play queue so that it will play again/next when it finishes. Since this is not expected behavior of Smart Shuffle it's worth looking in to. First I recommend making sure that you've not accidentally enabled Repeat - . If you've not already done this, if it's possible I also suggest checking if the same behavior persists in the app on another device or in the Web Player on your PC. If it doesn't happen then you can log out > back in twice in a row for a resync of the app and your account on the Windows device. As a more advanced step you can go for a clean reinstall which often helps resolve different performance issues and you can follow the steps here. Then you can download Spotify again either from our website or the MS store (the version that you weren't using so far). Appreciate the cooperation and keep us posted. IvelinaModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a "Like".Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!
2. Thank you for helpingRepeat is disabled.I did check it on my phone by connecting to the same play queue and adding the first suggested Smart play song. Then it is not added to the top of the queue again.Then I logged out and in 3x (first time I did not close the app, the 2nd and 3rd I did).And again added the first smart shuffle song which was being played to the the list and again it is added on top of the queue. See attached pictureBut I found a probable reason:One thing of difference of how I add a Smart Shuffle song on my phone and on my pc I just noticed:On my phone I can only add a song to the playlist using the + sign next to the song.In my pc app I:click the "..." next to the playing song in the queueselect "add to another playlist"select the current playlist to add it to.--> it gets added to the top of the list right after the current playing song as depicted in the picture.But when I select "Add to current playlist" that does not happen.In the past I often saw that adding a playing song to the current playlist did not work (nothing was added) or ended up in the "liked music" list (even when it was my own playlist). So I adopted the way I described above to make sure it gets added to the playlist I want to add it to.But still my question is: why should it be added to the top of the play queue when you add a song to "another" playlist (and that playlist happens to be the one you are playing)?Note: I did not check out what happens if I play playlist1 and add a song from that to playlist2 (so use above functionality to which it is intended). But also there it would make no sense to add the song to the top of the "playlist2" play queue, since that queue is not being played (the "playlist1" queue is being played).And in the past that did not lead to the song being readded to the play-queue (for that playlist). Only since I think a month I see that readding the song to the play-queue "issue".
3. Just tested adding a smart shuffle song really to another playlist. In that case it does not get duplicated on top of the current play-queue.
4. Hey @Nemelis, Thank you for keeping in contact and for all the info shared. In order to look into this further with the internal teams, let us know the following info: - Your device info (make/model) and OS version - Exact Spotify version We'll be waiting for your response. OscarDCModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!

Title: Other people can't see my public playlist

PlanPremiumCountryFinlandDevice(Samsung Galaxy A35 5g & HP laptop)Operating System(Android & Windows 11 home) My Question or IssueFor some reason other people can't see my playlist in my profile even though it's public and it's been added to my profile. It won't work even with a direct link to the playlist. Why is this? I've tried to remove it from the profile and add it back but that doesn't do anything. Also I've tried to make it private and then public but it still won't show. I'd appreciate any help. 

--- Community replies ---
1. Hi, @thirdxporsche, In that case it's possible that the system detected something unusual about your playlist and flagged it, making it only visible to you. I'd recommend checking your inbox for any recent emails from Spotify you may have received as there may be something in there which could give you a bit more insight on the matter. If the issue persists, it's worth making a new playlist and copying the contents of the old one into it (you can do so from the three dots menu of the old playlist > Add to other playlist > New playlist). After that, you can add it to your profile or share it with your friends and it should work. Cheers YordanModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution". Title: Spotify Duo mix disappeared

It's just disappeared. when I checked my saved playlists the name was here but with "this playlist deleted" text 

--- Community replies ---
1. Hi @mindbarometer, Thanks for posting in the Spotify Community! Unfortunately, Duo Mix was deprecated earlier this year and is no longer available, so it’s expected that you won’t be able to access this playlist. However, if you’d still like a shared playlist combining the music that both you and the other member of the Premium Duo plan listen to, you can instead create a Blend with them. If it sounds of interest, you can read more about Blends here. Let me know if you have any other questions, and have a good day! View solution in original post
2. Hi @mindbarometer, Thanks for posting in the Spotify Community! Unfortunately, Duo Mix was deprecated earlier this year and is no longer available, so it’s expected that you won’t be able to access this playlist. However, if you’d still like a shared playlist combining the music that both you and the other member of the Premium Duo plan listen to, you can instead create a Blend with them. If it sounds of interest, you can read more about Blends here. Let me know if you have any other questions, and have a good day!
3. Hi, Thanks for the info, I'll try Blends But what's the point of the Duo supsbcription now? Only money saving?
4. Hi @mindbarometer, Thank you for your reply! That’s right, the point of a Premium Duo (or Premium Family) subscription is the ability to have multiple users who live together enjoying Premium features at a discounted price. Let me know if you have any other questions, and have a good day!

review_text:
Title: Slower, but glad that Spotify isn’t advertising for ICE anymore
Rating: 3 Stars

Spotify playback is really lagging: songs, artist profiles, playlists, and podcasts are all consistently loading much slower than they used to. For how much of a drag this is, I think premium subscriptions are overpriced. I’m very glad that Spotify ceased advertising ICE recruitment ads, because I boycotted Spotify as a result of that (and encouraged others to do the same!) Once Spotify stopped airing advertisements for ICE recruitment, I did return to the platform because I enjoy accessing my playlists and podcasts from the same app. That said, now that I’ve returned, user experience has diminished due to the slowness and decrease in playback quality. Also, I wish artists were paid fairly for their work!

review_text:
Title: Reached My Limit
Rating: 2 Stars

I’ve been using Spotify since 2020 and used to love it. I used to be very adamant about never changing music apps, but I’m at my limit. 30 seconds used to be LONG for ad breaks, but now they’ve been reaching two minutes. I also used to be able to listen to 5-7 songs straight without ads, but now it’s more 3-4–5 if I’m REALLY lucky. 

Music is one of my biggest coping mechanisms, however I cant even have 15 minutes to myself without an ad. In fact, recently, they’ve changed it so ads will play right after the first song when you decide to start playing music. Not to mention the additional non-audio ads that pop up around the app as well. 

Now, the contents of these ads is another topic entirely. I’m not a purist by any means, but I really don’t enjoy hearing ads sampling a suggestive piece of a smutty audiobook, ESPECIALLY when I’m with family. 

If I hate the ads so much, why don’t I just buy premium? I used to, but then the price rose. And again, and again, and again. It is now THIRTEEN U.S. DOLLARS (fine, $12.99) for a single person. That is ridiculous. 

Additionally, the yearly music wraps have significantly decreased in quality. They used to be fun and accurate, but now parts are very obviously incorrect, nor do they seem to capture activity from the entire year. This is a big disappointment because, as someone who prides themself in listening to a wide range of genres, I loved seeing which ones I had explored each year. However, I began to see less appear in my “wraps” despite knowing very well I had listened to more. Whether it’s due to Spotify shrinking/simplifying genre groups or a lack of effort, I may never know, but it’s frustrating regardless.

Originally I had contemplated one star, but the fact that Spotify does offer a free listening experience grants it another. Nowadays it feels like you have to give an arm and a leg just for the bare minimum, but thankfully this wide range of music isn’t behind a paywall… just yet.

review_text:
Title: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Rating: 5 Stars

Spotify is one of the most popular music streaming apps in the world, offering millions of songs, podcasts, and playlists in one platform. The app is very easy to use, with a clean interface that allows users to quickly search for music, discover new artists, and create their own playlists.

One of Spotify’s strongest features is its recommendation system. It suggests songs based on your listening habits, which makes discovering new music enjoyable and personalized. The app also supports offline listening for premium users, which is useful when you don’t have internet access.

However, the free version includes ads and some limitations like shuffle-only playback on mobile. Despite that, the audio quality is good and the overall experience is smooth.

Overall, Spotify is an excellent music streaming app for both casual listeners and music lovers.
