review_text:
Title: Question of the Week: Do you remember the first song you listened to on Spotify?

It's time for another Question of the Week! Over the past month, I have been listening to a song that I listened and saved when I first opened my Spotify account back in 2015. This song makes me relive joyful memories and the upbeat tone associated with this song. To that end, I wanted to see what the Community has to say about this. What is the first song you've listened to on Spotify and do you still listen to it? Here's mine: https://open.spotify.com/track/6Uy6K3KdmUdAfelUp0SeXn?si=6eaedd4e2fe84f8e Looking forward to your responses! Billy-JSpotify StarHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate my answer, maybe give me a Like.Note: I'm not a Spotify employee.

--- Community replies ---
1. Excellent question @Billy-J! The beginning is always special! I first started jamming to some classic metal bangers and would listen to some jazz playlists while at work, using the wide variety to discover some new things. spotify:playlist:37i9dQZF1DXbITWG1ZJKYt I would've thought that my first song would be from Metallica or something, but the recent Party of the Year reminded me, that it was in fact a band I saw play alongside Metallica - Gojira! It was namely this song, which was brand new back in 2016 when I made my account. spotify:track:5uunXHE4kIW6uS4HWAXaOQ:small JoanModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!
2. I love this question, this is the first song I listened to in Spotify https://open.spotify.com/track/70e11mvW5B84cnVumxwRnY?si=hsbW1waST2ejuOtKTBlBcg I was getting ready to see the band at a local bar. I'm lucky I got to see them in such a close and intimate environment. They got big in Colombia and only do big concerts now. This and many of their songs are still in my rotation Anyk17
3. I don’t remember the exact very first track I played on Spotify, but I do remember the early days were full of whatever I was obsessed with at the time, just hitting play and letting the algorithm run. Funny thing is, a couple of those songs still pop up in my playlists today and instantly take me back to that “new Spotify account” phase. It’s interesting how a song can anchor a whole period of life like that.
4. One of the first songs I saved on Spotify was "Counting Stars" by OneRepublic, and yes, I still listen to it! Every time it comes on, it brings back memories from when I first started using Spotify. It's amazing how certain songs can instantly take you back to a specific time in your life. https://ppcinneapk.com.br/

review_text:
Title: Paying for playlist placements

Hi everyone,Hopefully this is the correct category for this questionIn the Spotify's terms of service it says:'Any service that claims to offer guaranteed placement on playlists on Spotify in exchange for money are in violation of our terms & conditions, and they shouldn't be used.'. However, services like Submithub are allowed right? Where you pay the playlister to listen to your song and add it if they like it.Now I have been emailing some playlist curators (by checking the email they have in the description of their playlists) and sometimes it's quite clear that they are going against the regulations. However sometimes I get a reply akin to: "We listened to your song and it sounds like a great fit, we normally ask for a donation of $xx,xx would you like to be placed?" I'm wondering if this is legit, am I allowed to 'donate' to a playlister when they claim to like my song? Also, are there ways to check whether the playlist has legitimate followers or whether they are only fake accounts?Yours sincerely,Sander

--- Community replies ---
1. Hey there @SanderKaatee, Thanks for reaching out about this here in the Community. We can confirm that this kind of behavior isn't allowed and Spotify doesn't collect money for playlist placement. If you'd like to report this, you can reach out directly to our Support team through any of these channels so they can further assist you with this. If you need a hand with anything else, the Community is here for you. EniModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate my answer, maybe give me a Like. “Music acts like a magic key, to which the most tightly closed heart opens.”– Maria von Trapp
2. So the donation based playlists are also against the guidelines? Even when they claim that 'they've listened to the song and think its suitable'?
3. I am also really confused by this. I found a list of playlist curators through DistroKid’s website. When I contacted them, they wanted to place me on a playlist but charged me $35 to be there. Have I done something wrong? I will report and take down my song from the list if this is not allowed. Someone please explain so I don’t do something that breaks rules - I am the first person to respect and follow TOS on any website, but I simply am so confused as a new artist that I can’t determine if I have.
4. Hi there @ajspraguemusic, Thanks for reaching out. Spotify’s User Guidelines (Section 7) prohibit users from accepting or offering to accept any compensation, financial or otherwise, to influence the content included on an account or playlist (to learn more click here). If you notice this type of activity and/or have questions about whether a particular playlist offer violates Spotify’s terms, you can report it by contacting our Support team through any of these channels. We thank our users for their cooperation. AlexModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!

review_text:
Title: How do I turn off the ai dj

The ai dj aromatically turned on but I don't want it, does anyone know how to get rid of it? 

--- Community replies ---
1. Hey there, Thanks for posting in the Community and welcome. In this case, we'd like to add some insights. Listening to DJ only happens when you press play. To listen, you just need to head to Home, click on the DJ card and press play (you can also find it in your Library). If you don't want to use the feature, you just need to press stop, exit out of it, or hit play on another track, playlist, podcast or audiobook. That way you won't listen to the DJ. In case you were looking for a way to turn off or change the DJ voice, note that this hasn't been implemented yet, but there's a live idea on the topic which you may find interesting, so feel free to check it out and show your support. Hope this clears things up. If you have any questions or need anything else, the Community is here for you.

review_text:
Title: Why do daily mixes keep disappearing?

PlanPremiumCountryNew ZealandDeviceWindows 10 My Question or IssueI regularly use the Made for you/Daily Mixes to generate a random list of things I have liked or have played before. I use them to generate my own main playlist which I use everyday. But I found it frustrating that the Spotify UI keep changing and made these features hard to find. They used to be available on the homepage, then they were relegated to a button on the search tab, and now they appear to have gone completely? What is going on?I had noticed the daily mixes appeared to be less random than they used to be, and often repeated the same songs. Would it be possible to have a setting (like a slider) that one could move between "More variety > Less Variety" - musicmatch player used to do this in the MP3 days and it was great.Another way of solving the problem of having the Daily Mixes constantly move in the UI would be to allow people to customised their own home page.If you have removed the Daily Mixes, can you please reinstate a feature that allows you to create a random list based on your previous listening. It should not be that hard - without such a feature I may consider going to another service.Joe

--- Community replies ---
1. The Daily Mixes appeared on my home screen again today - yay, but for how long I did experiment with SmartShuffle, however I was surprised that it kept choosing the same songs to add to the upnext playlist, why would I want to hear something again from only a few tracks ago. Just checking that the song it is about to add is not already on the queue would be an easy tweak to make to the algorithm?
2. I was going back to some of my mixes and started retracking them This one is completely deleted https://open.spotify.com/playlist/37i9dQZF1EQp62d3Dl7ECY
3. Hi there, @Infinitmon Thanks for reaching out and letting us know about this! Keep in mind that the Daily Mix playlists learn what the user loves and offer up an evolving set of content based on their listening habits. Instead of being centered around music that’s new to you, Daily Mix is built around your favorite songs and songs you listen to frequently. In that case, if you don't use the app regularly the Daily mix doesn't get refreshed as it should (even though it doesn't refresh daily the songs included in each mix get updated roughly once a week). That might be the reason why the Daily Mix playlist you saved before is now empty. We suggest using the app frequently and also not saving the Daily mix playlist itself if you like it, because it can change from time to time. Instead, you can create a new playlist on your own and select the songs you want from the Daily Mix or select all of them (you can do that with CTRL/CMD+A on the desktop app) and copy them in that new playlist you've created. The Daily Mix playlists should always appear under the Made For You section of the app. Here you can find more info about all of this. Hope this clears things up! If you have any other doubts, the Community will be here for you! Paula_ViModeratorHelp others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution".If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like.Are you new to the Community? Take a moment to introduce yourself!
